name: Cleveland-Cliffs Inc.

profile.description: Cleveland-Cliffs is the largest flat-rolled steel producer in North America, operating through steelmaking, downstream manufacturing, and tooling. Revenue grew 6.3% YoY. CEO Lourenco Goncalves. Headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Cliffs acquired ArcelorMittal USA and AK Steel, transforming from iron ore miner to integrated steelmaker. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI. Market Share (2/2): #1 flat-rolled steel producer in North America. Product Pipeline (1/2): Automotive steel focus. R&D Intensity (0/1): Below average.

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (1/4): Industrial purpose. Community Impact (1/2): Major U.S. employer. Employee Retention (1/2): Strong union relationships. Insider Buying (0/2): CEO Goncalves is outspoken advocate for domestic steel.

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Revenue grew 6.3% but profit margin of -6.4% and ROE of -18.6% reflect losses [1]. D/E of 129.3% above target. Forward P/E of 27.4.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Steel manufacturing is physical and capital-intensive, structurally immune to AI. AI data center construction increases steel demand. AI Opportunity (1/1): Automation in steel production.

infinite_game.summary: CEO Goncalves runs CLF with a combative, finite-game approach focused on tariff lobbying, aggressive M&A, and antagonizing competitors.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (0/3): No purpose beyond domestic steel dominance. Goncalves's combative style is quintessentially finite.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): Goncalves publicly attacks competitors and critics.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Iron ore to steel transformation.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (0/2): Combative approach is about winning, not courage.

investment_thesis: CLF is the #1 flat-rolled steel producer in North America with Section 232 tariff protection and 6.3% revenue growth. $13.78/share may represent cyclical trough value.
thesis_continued: Persistent losses (ROE -18.6%, profit margin -6.4%), D/E of 129%, and Goncalves's volatile management style create significant risk [1].
key_catalysts: Steel price recovery from cyclical trough
key_risks: CEO governance concerns — combative style alienates investors
Chinese steel overcapacity and import competition
sources: Yahoo Finance — CLF Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
